title: Coleocephalocereus pleurocarpus
taxonomy: Coleocephalocereus pleurocarpus is classified as a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Coleocephalocereus, which is a group of columnar cacti native to South America. The species is further classified under the subfamily Pereskioideae. As part of the broader Cactaceae family, Coleocephalocereus pleurocarpus is a member of the order Caryophyllales. The classification of Coleocephalocereus pleurocarpus is a key aspect of understanding its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: The Coleocephalocereus pleurocarpus, also known as the "fairy castle" cactus, is a species of cactus native to Mexico, characterized by its unique, clustered growth habit and short, cylindrical stems. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Coleocephalocereus pleurocarpus include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, particularly those related to the respiratory and gastrointestinal systems.
